title: EvidenceFinding
description: A single piece of evidence collected by a worker. Referenced by hypotheses, AI evaluation, and the final report via evidenceId.

evidenceId.description: Stable identifier for this finding. Must be unique within an incident. Referenced by hypotheses, AI outputs, and the final report (e.g. ev-metrics-001).

resourceType.description: Type of AWS resource this finding relates to (e.g. api-gateway, rds-instance, lambda).

resourceName.description: Name of the specific resource (e.g. checkout-service, orders-db).

findingType.description: Machine-readable finding category (e.g. latency_spike, error_log_spike, trace_latency_spike).

summary.description: Human-readable one-line description of the finding. Used in report evidence highlights.

score.description: Relevance or anomaly score for this finding (0.0–1.0). Higher = more significant. Optional but recommended.

details.description: Source-specific structured data. For logs findings on ERROR/Exception patterns, may include an optional 'exceptionSamples' field: a list of up to 3 concrete exception message strings (each ≤200 chars) sampled from the incident window. The Candidate Hypotheses Builder reads exceptionSamples to enrich the application error surge cause label with the dominant exception class name (display-only, does not affect confidence).
